response.text:
# REFLECTION

Household r0c2 is in acute energy distress while islanded: SoC has dropped to 13.11/39 kWh (33.6%), with a 1.0 kW load consuming the battery at ~7.5 hours of autonomy remaining. The cluster-wide crisis is evident—r0c1, r0c3, and r1c2 are all sending repeated urgent and normal requests, indicating systemic undersupply. The previous policy's 35% sharing threshold has proven unsustainable; r0c2 is now *below* that threshold and cannot afford to share without accelerating its own collapse. Critically, r1c2 explicitly rejected r0c2's offer, citing its own survival buffer (37%), which signals that even peers in crisis are rationing. The pattern of rejections and repeated requests suggests no generation recovery is imminent. In islanded mode with no trust circles and no external support visible, r0c2 must shift to strict self-preservation: suspend all outbound sharing until SoC recovers above 50%, reject incoming requests to protect the remaining 13 kWh, and monitor for any sign of reconnection or local generation. Continuing to share at 0.15 kW/tick would deplete the battery in ~87 ticks (~22 minutes), leaving no margin for load spikes or communication delays.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.50
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Islanded crisis mode. SoC 33.6% and falling; autonomy ~7.5 hours at 1 kW load. Cluster-wide undersupply confirmed by peer rejections and repeated requests. Previous 35% threshold unsustainable. Suspend all sharing until SoC ≥ 50%. Prioritize self-preservation and load survival. Monitor for generation recovery or reconnection."
ttl_ticks: 10
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
